key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 forms a denser, more protective oxide scale than detonation-sprayed T-400, giving better resistance to molten solar-salt corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal storage containers, HVAF T-400 coatings appear more durable than DS coatings under long-term nitrate-salt exposure.
method: Tribaloy-400 coatings were deposited on ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying.
Samples were exposed to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten solar salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Microstructure, phase formation, elemental mapping, mass gain, and spallation were compared.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that HVAF retained a uniform Cr–Mo–Si–O enriched surface with minimal salt penetration after 1000 h, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated under one molten-salt composition and temperature, so coating selection may need revalidation for different CSP salts, thermal cycles, or service conditions.
paper_title: Hot corrosion behavior of thermal sprayed using HVAF and DS techniques with Tribaloy-400 coatings for solar energy storage container
